reviews.review_description: I quite like this, but on first use after purchase (after the free trial) the game glitched significantly during the rally bit. I was on the unlimited ball one where the ball machine will “rarely” miss and the machine came through the net so it could never return. It also would throw out a ball that I hit which then disappeared and a second immediately came out. Disappointing when it cost £12 to buy

reviews.review_title: Helped me improve my irl game
reviews.review_description:
This is hands down the best tennis simulation on quest. I play irl and been doing a couple of drills. 
It helped me improve my timing immensely and work on a couple shot.Had some of my best games irl after buying this!
Obviously there is room to improve in UI in general , some sliced shots, maybe add some more drills but overall this is the eleventable equivalent for tennis.

Amazingly realistic physics. This is the best ball machine and practice sim for real tennis I could imagine. Get a racket attachment for your controller and get ready for some awesome fully customizable swing drills and the best ball machine ever. Never have to pick up balls again.  I can’t believe a game of this quality doesn’t have a bigger following. This alone makes the entire purchase of a meta quest worth it and is in fact essentially the only thing I use my quest for.

The talent of the coder/designer is apparent at first glance. The user interface and design are clean and functional.  The free edition has a lot to offer, and I was still hooked after 2 hours. I was challenged to play online, but I did not feel ready.
I use my own paddle, the jumpy ball can be changed in rebound and string grip settings. This flexibility has immense potential and should be extended.
I practised drills with various grips, ranging from continental to semi-western, and was pleasantly delighted how well it transferred real timing and sensation. However, e-sports drills feel better.
Playing a game is a different story. It is really difficult, even more so than real life. It offers a complete autorun option as well as a joystick option for movement. 
I try to run without using the joystick in the portal passthrough mode, a box carved out of VR that allows me to stay aware of my surroundings. But lines and balls become invisible left & right outside the box. When I run forward through the portal, I exit mixed reality and enter full VR. Weird passing into the Twilight Zone, lol. But the court constantly repositions with each serve, making free running risky and unplayable.
The passthrough venue option allows to freely run without a limit. Setting the game to random speed and spin, somehow the rebounds and spins feel exaggerated, frustratingly hard to read and anticipate position. This aspect of the game needs most consideration in updates. Meanwhile I challenge a bot who has no racket with  low spins. Give the bot a racket! 
The free version is worth the 15€, support this game, even if it has a way to go. I bought it to configure random left to right real run drills, that get me nicely sweating in a full passthrough view, with a full coloured tennis surface beyond the wall of my living room. Amazing! The game deserves a drill video on my YT VR Tennis channel, along with Esport, which freerun is limited guardian size. In 2025, the best 2 VR tennis sims.
Definitely playable but needs improvement if it is to catch up to some other titles.

Court Time Tennis has a generous, free trial you can’t refuse. CTT was created by a passionate tennis player and sim expert. It has two mixed reality modes for small and large spaces and the best physics of any racquet game I’ve played. It takes you through lots of drills to improve your skills. For someone who hasn’t played IRL tennis since high school (me), this is a great learning game. But it is also good for those who just want to get into the swing of things for a little activity/exercise or are competitive with online multiplayer. And if you enjoy the game, it’s only $14.99 to go pro!

Court Time Tennis has the best physics of any racquet game that I have played.   I played irl tennis almost every day for many years until I got too old to do all that running.   Now having this game to play is like a shot of youth and it gives me the opportunity to  hone my strokes like never before.  It sure is fun to do that.
I have not tried multiplayer. But as single player, this is my favourite.

reviews.review_title: Best vr Tennis Sim, by far
reviews.review_description:
I have tried them all, this is the most realistic vr Tennis Sim for me, no other comes close.
The most Important thing, besides the incredible physics is the possibility to customize the racket physics and adjust to your own racket and feel. I am not aware of any other vr Tennis game allowing this. 

Playing only with Helloreal 47 (its a sim, why use a Controller only?), i have no issues transitioning between vr and RL, and since i played this sim my real life Tennis is more consistent than ever before

The Training Options are great and very well implemented, the ball machine is a thrill. 

I only play moving free to keep.it as real as possible in Portal mode or full passthrough. The game has Options that respect your own available space. 

It also offers online matches and offline against a bot. 
You can start with a free to play Mode without risk to test. 
The developer responds on discord and i recommend to join that channel for Information and Feedback and suggestions. Also to get help if you struggle in the beginning 

The game is growing and constantly being improved and still getting better. 

5 Stars without a doubt

I just downloaded this after sawing the post on reddit and was so impressed by the free version I immediately bought the  full version. Its the perfect workout for me during winter. I usually use a semi western grip but my ball keeps hitting downward, which i assume is because i m a bit rusty. It would be great if there is a replay feature, where after a drill we can watch the slow motion replay of our racket and swing path to see our contact point and angle.

This app has excellent realism and choice of visual options for the court settings and racket. I particularly enjoy the option when playing the bot which shows the trail of the coming ball so I can use the joystick to move on the court and thus simulate better real life playing. I hope the app improves to simulate a realistic person instead of a bot machine.......

I’ve tried every single VR tennis game, this is by far the best one if you want a near close to real life tennis simulation. It’s almost close to perfect in that regard, and unlike the others which will give you bad muscle memory habits, this can give you great practice. I’m giving it 5 stars because it’s amazing in that regard, but it’s not perfect and there are things I wish it had, which who knows, it’s still in development and one day might, but even if it never does, I’ll be happy practicing.

One thing I wish it had which I think may be possible some day, is add the great haptic feedback another game has which you can really feel even through the handle adapter and it almost feels like there’s weight when you’re hitting the ball and it adds to the realism. I’m not sure if it’s just increasing the vibration, maybe it could have a setting so you can set how much you want depending on your real racquet, similar to bounce. The sound may also be playing a part in that experience.

Another thing is, I wish you could play against an opponent that looks like a person instead of just a machine chucking the ball back so you can also practice reading their shots. One game has a “Mirror mode” that let’s you practice against yourself and it’s fun and lets you see how your shot placement looks and can affect an opponent and the only reason I ever boot it up.

The other thing which it may never have as it may not be the purpose of the game is a career mode that let’s you go to different tournaments and venues and you can increase your rank based on where you place as you get points throughout your career. I can’t tell you how different it feels to practice when you’re really under pressure trying to win a tournament, they’re up 30-40 and your turn to serve. It gives you mental practice and also the various stages of a tennis match. If it had those things, it would be beyond perfect, but as I said, even without it, this is the go to game for anyone that loves tennis.
After 3d printing a racket adapter this game is working really well for me. Keep up the great work. Kind of blown away really.

Pretty decent game so far but I find it very irritating that in rally practice mode the ball bot keeps missing my shots (especially hard shots) and then it resets (ie. there's a pause while it does some pointless applause before it gets going again).  Therefore there is no rally, just one or two shots.  Please make it so the ball bot always returns my shots or if it misses my shots it fires a new ball out straight away to continue the rally.  

Some other improvements would be a button to auto toss the ball for serving because I play with one controller due to having a double handed backhand I have to throw and hit the ball with the same controller which make for a unnatural serve motion.  I would also love some sort of replay function to check my strokes.  As a real tennis player I want to see that my strokes are correct and that I'm always looking at the ball at contact.  Finally, I like there's some audio feedback when you hit the ball hard, as far as I can tell there is no audio feedback when you frame the ball so that would be good to add.  

Generally a decent game and I much preferred this to the other tennis games available but it just needs some minor improvements/additions to make it perfect.  I hope it continues development and has a successful release.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback!  The rally mode is designed as a challenge for you to keep up a rally with the bot, not hit past it.  But I've just added bot speed parameter that you can increase, and also a new Unlimited Rally that works as you hoped.  I've put your other good ideas to our Discord members, please join the conversation there!

reviews.review_description:
This is the most realistic tennis game I have played. And the standard version is free! You get numerous 'game drills' and 'warm up rallies' against a bot to test your skills. Each one has a 'star rating' to encourage you to improve.
There are a range of customizable settings. You can play in different venues and on different surfaces to name just two. 
Each week there is a new leaderbaord to see how you fare against others.
You can also match play against a bot with increasing levels of difficulty.
If you upgrade to the Pro mode then even more settings are configurable for each drill. So ball speed, wind speed, spin, pace and even racket bounce and grip styles.
The Pro mode also allows you to access the multiplayer mode and take on players from around the world.
Loads of fun.

The move circle thing is so ducking
 useless . It acts like it knows my room better than me. Every single time i try to catch a ball and bend over , the screen turns black like my f***ing d***k. This game is fluid but it has the most useless additions in the world. They act like you dont need to move when you play tennis. Every single one of you that doesnt move are fat f***ks.

developer_response.body: I created the foot circle because your swings can reach the boundary of your room faster than the guardian can show them.  It is turned off in portal mode where you can see your actual room, did you try that?

reviews.review_description:
Missing tennis ball in the game drill for serves drill. Unable to play this serves game drill.  (Updated review)

First, thank you developers for this VR mixed reality tennis game, I enjoyed playing it very much.
Please continue to add more gameplay , more game options , more court indoor and outdoor environments, 
More mixed reality and pass through options to play. For example floor pass through option. 
and more different levels of AI players ( normal,  hard, advanced,  pro AI levels) to this game if possible.  
Please continue to improve the auto run function and the physical movement of the tennis ball.  
Yes, i will purchase the pro level to play more.  And more please.

First, thank you developers for this VR mixed reality tennis game, I enjoyed playing it very much.
Please continue to add more gameplay , more game options , more court indoor and outdoor environments, 
More mixed reality and pass through options to play. For example floor pass through option. 
and more different levels of AI players ( normal,  hard, advanced,  pro AI levels) to this game if possible.  
Please continue to improve the auto run function and the physical movement of the tennis ball.  
Yes, i will purchase the pro level to play more.  And more please.

developer_response.body: You're welcome!  We have many of those features and improvements in the works.  Floor passthrough is available now with the "Passthrough Venue", and you can unlock difficulty settings and customizable drills with the Pro Pass.

reviews.review_description:
This tennis app is the most fun I've had in VR, ever.
The many shot drills are addictive and give you a light workout while improving your shots. The controls feel great, letting you hit topspin and slice shots just like the real thing. You can even attach the controller to your racket frame for additional realism.
Highly recommend this for players of all ages.
#tennisathome

reviews.review_title: Getting back into the swing of things!
reviews.review_description: Haven't played tennis since highschool (seems like 100 years ago) but I can't think of a more enjoyable way to get back into this sport.  Definitely a little rusty at first, but once I started to intuit the feel of the game without the weight of a physical racket in my hands, I quickly got the hang of things.  Now I just need a larger space to play for both forehand and backhand swings with confidence.  Overall a fantastic tennis experience and can't wait to see what gets added to the app by the developer.  Great job!

I'm a tennis player, and have played for over 35yrs! I've played at a v high level in my younger years and always wished there was a good tennis computer game.
Wii Tennis got me excited but was such a letdown.
I've just tried this. All assists off straight away. It's a nice little game for sure. I would say that the physics are pretty good.
However it's missing haptic feedback when you strike the ball. This is mega important especially when you are applying spin on the ball. 
I doubt it will be hard to add this feature in.
Will be interesting to see how this game will progress. Keep up the good work!

developer_response.body: Thanks for checking it out!  There's a haptic response but perhaps tuned too low.  It changes haptic amplitude/frequence/period based on hit speed, but doesn't max out until pretty high speeds with just a controller.  It maxes out it quicker if you're using a real racket or extender, so will try that all the time.

reviews.review_description:
The physics for a tennis sim feel quite good. You can really feel the difference when putting different spin and power on the hits. Well done!

(I love the little robot too.)

developer_response.body: The "Ball Bot" is our favorite too.  Long live robots!

reviews.review_description: Not a huge tennis fan but this game is a lot of fun to play around with. The physics feel great and the drills actually seemed to improve my backhand in about 20 minutes.

reviews.review_title: Great training for real life tennis
reviews.review_description:
I've been playing real tennis almost every day this year, but now that winter has come I need to turn to VR. I've tried several other apps and this one seems to be the best for people interested in training and improving their real tennis skills, as opposed to playing and having fun arcade style.

Wishlist
- change the default racket bounce to something that behaves like an average racket. With the current default the amount of power generated is absurdly high, making it impossible to rally from the service line. I almost dismissed the app as completely useless until I realized this is a setting I can change. After setting it way down to 0.4, I started getting a similar feel to real life, but now I'm not sure if I didn't overdo it and I would need to go to a real court and hit a few balls to calibrate my feel.
- whenever a drill has a setting for forehand, backhand, etc. etc. and random, add an extra option to choose randomly but only between forehand and backhand. All those other options that involve artificial movement feel terrible to me and I want to avoid them. This should be very easy to implement and would make the app a lot more useful for me.

Thank you for the great work and please keep it up!

I regularly play tennis with retired pros, college players, and club monsters.  I'm subscribed to The Tennis Channel, TennisTV, and I attend pro tournaments to spectate when I can get a chance.  Needless to say, I love tennis.  And If you love tennis like me you will love Court Time Tennis.  It features the most true to life physics I've ever seen in a tennis game and has a ton of settings you can mess with if you want to simulate different scenarios.  The shot assist mode was so good at interpreting my ground strokes that it took me a few minutes to even realize I was being assisted, and the unassisted mode feels more realistic than I previously thought was possible for this generation of VR.  Directional ball spin actually matters as well as velocity. The developer is constantly updating and open to feedback so I am very excited to see this game grow.  At the moment the game is geared toward singles play drills but there is an excellent multiplayer mode, wall hitting mode, and a moving ball machine bot that simulates competition.  I'm sure there will be more in the future so I'm not really too concerned.  Considering the game is only in beta at the time of my writing this, Court Time Tennis is a 10/10 for me.
The drills, match play, spin, hit speed, and play experience are all exceptional. You will lose yourself in this game. Also a tremendous training aid. We’ll done!
This game is great.  The physics feel incredibly good and the mini-games are a ton of fun.  Super excited to see how this game evolves.  Highly recommend it!

Fair priced pro version included more realistic wind and angle
options.
Tennis game with the best ball physics, I tried all relevant tennis
games. First Person Tennis is also very good, tournaments
but not so good physics.
IMO developer should improve:
- auto teleportation option for relaxed games
- AI tournaments
- ball physic option for an arcade mode with faster, harder workout 
matches. Example is the arcade mode in Racket Fury table tennis. 
This is a masterpiece for fast playing, unbelievable.

At all: Congratulation for this work and enough power for improvements

developer_response.body: Thanks for the ideas!  I'm planning more gaming modes soon

reviews.review_title: Most realistic fluid tennis sim so far
reviews.review_description:
From the dev of ProShot tennis which had much promise. Already with the wall, passthrough, robot, wind physics etc this is elevating it above the competition.

There are some good tennis games now and I've played them all. This feels the most fluid and natural in terms of swing and physics. A very talented dev well worth supporting.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the encouragement!  Lots more to come

It's free to play the beginning drills and rallies and practice game with the bot. All options are enabled including MR portal, passthrough venue, etc.  There's an in-game Pro upgrade to unlock the rest, including ranked bot and live matches and build-your-own-drills.  You can try that upgrade free for 1 day, and after that buy for $14.99

reviews.review_description:
I thought this was too good to be free, but upon starting the game, my avatar was stuck with its arms spread out where I should be standing. This got in my way of my sight a little and is just otherwise distracting out-of-virtual-body experience.. The physics and general movement were good though.
I stopped playing once I seen the pay wall pop up that basically keeps you from doing much of anything at all. I'm not holding that against it, as the devs deserve money for their efforts, I just had my hopes up. 😅
I don't see myself paying for the title as is though, and it may just not be for me.

developer_response.body: Not sure what happened to your avatar!  There's a new release with many improvements that may fix.  Feel free to join our Discord (https://courttimetennis.com/discord) for support!

reviews.review_description: I’ve been playing this game for a while now and mostly enjoy…however recently there seems to be a glitch….if you hit a lob high it comes down on the bot if you’re close bounces away then the bot spews a ball out….its not right. I only play the practice mode so it’s in the three set match….otherwise it’s good. Lose a star….worth the money though.

developer_response.body: Thanks, I'll try to reproduce that bug. Join us on Discord to give more feedback!

reviews.review_description:
Open court is just plain fun. There's a lot you have control over, the different spin you can put on the ball. It's also a very generous free version with all kinds of drills and rallies you compete with others in.
The physics are top tier. In the manner of walkabout, eleven, and crazy kung Fu.

Impressive. Get it. While playing against the wall, several times I'll have several balls left to use but it won't let me have another ball. Like, ill click with the off controller and nothing appears in my hand.
I've tried all the tennis games available on the Meta Store. Unsurprisingly, this game offers the best tennis gameplay sensation and provides a lot of enjoyment.

Very good game so far, I'm on the free version so I haven't accessed everything. My only real complaint is that during the wall game mode sometimes the ball will just disappear and won't allow you to keep going, so if you have a high score it ends up being pointless which is frustrating. Besides that great game.

developer_response.body: We hadn't heard about that bug before and will try to reproduce it!  If you can post a video of it to our Discord we might learn what triggers it.

reviews.review_description: For an app in development this seems really good. Physics feel nice and realistic at first play. Not sure how good this is to improve real tennis but I'm impressed enough to stick with it and find out.

developer_response.body: Thanks for saying so!  I'm excited to further improve and add new features.

reviews.review_title: Great game but one problem for me.
reviews.review_description: It’s a great game but the contact between the tennis racket and the ball are a little off for me. Every time I hit the ball with a a light swing, the ball just blasts away. Even when I turned the tennis racket bounce all the way down I still had the same problem.

developer_response.body: We improved contact physics in a recent update, give it another try!  You can also tune your racket bounce and string friction now.

reviews.review_description: Great Game!!!! 🎾

I usually do not write reviews, but this was fun. I am not a tennis player by any means, but I felt that the physics of the spin on the ball were pretty spot on. How it might help improve your game I don't know. For this 53-year-old, it has made tennis fun.
